amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Tryptophan biosynthesis in E. coli is a 5-step pathway from chorismate (shikimate pathway product) requiring 7 enzymes encoded by the trp operon. The pathway: chorismate + glutamine → anthranilate (TrpE/D) → phosphoribosyl-anthranilate (TrpD) → CDRP → indole-3-glycerol phosphate (TrpC) → tryptophan (TrpA/B). TrpB combines indole from TrpA with serine to form tryptophan. The trp operon is regulated by TrpR repressor (binds tryptophan) and attenuation (TRAP mechanism with leader sequence).

graph TD %% Precursor from Shikimate A(\1) --> B[Shikimate Pathway Product] B --> C[TrpE/D Anthranilate Synthase] %% Step 1: Anthranilate Formation C --> D{AND: Chorismate + Gln
AND TrpE/D?} D -->|No| E[\Synthesis Blocked/] D -->|Yes| F[TrpE Amidotransferase] F --> G[Glutamine → Glutamate + NH3] G --> H[NH3 Attacks Chorismate] H --> I[Anthranilate Formation] I --> J[Pyruvate Released] %% Step 2: Phosphoribosylation I --> K[TrpD Phosphoribosyltransferase] K --> L{AND: Anthranilate + PRPP
AND TrpD?} L -->|No| E L -->|Yes| M[PRPP Transfer] M --> N[N-5'-Phosphoribosyl-anthranilate] N --> O[PPi Released] %% Step 3: Isomerization N --> P[TrpF Phosphoribosylanthranilate Isomerase] P --> Q[Amadori Rearrangement] Q --> R[1-O-Carboxyphenylamino-1-deoxyribulose-5-P] R --> S(\1) %% Step 4: Indole-Glycerol-P Formation S --> T[TrpC Indole-3-Glycerol-P Synthase] T --> U[Ring Closure + Decarboxylation] U --> V[Indole-3-Glycerol Phosphate] V --> W[CO2 Released] %% Step 5: Tryptophan Synthase V --> X[TrpA/B Tryptophan Synthase] X --> Y{AND: TrpA α-Subunit
AND TrpB β-Subunit?} Y -->|No| E Y -->|Yes| Z[α2β2 Tetramer] %% TrpA Reaction Z --> AA["TrpA: IGP → Indole + G3P"] AA --> AB[Indole Intermediate] AB --> AC[Indole Channeled to TrpB] %% TrpB Reaction AC --> AD["TrpB: Serine Dehydration"] AD --> AE[/Serine + PLP → Aminoacrylate/] AE --> AF[Indole + Aminoacrylate] AF --> AG[C-C Bond Formation] AG --> AH(\1) AH --> AI[PLP Regenerated] %% Regulation - TrpR Repressor AH --> AJ[Tryptophan Accumulation] AJ --> AK{OR: Trp
Level?} AK -->|High| AL[Trp Binds TrpR] AK -->|Low| AM[\TrpR Inactive/] AL --> AN[TrpR-Trp Complex] AN --> AO[TrpR Binds trp Operator] AO --> AP[/trp Operon Repression/] AP --> AQ[/No Transcription/] AM --> AR[/No Repression/] AR --> AS[trp Operon Transcription] AS --> AT[TrpEDCBA mRNA] AT --> AU[Enzyme Synthesis] AU --> C %% Attenuation Control AS --> AV[trpL Leader Sequence] AV --> AW{OR: Trp-tRNA
Charged?} AW -->|Yes - High Trp| AX[Ribosome Fast Through Leader] AW -->|No - Low Trp| AY[Ribosome Stalls at Trp Codons] AX --> AZ["Stem-Loop 3:4 Forms"] AZ --> BA[/Terminator Hairpin/] BA --> BB[/Transcription Terminated/] BB --> AQ AY --> BC["Stem-Loop 2:3 Forms"] BC --> BD[/Antiterminator/] BD --> BE[Transcription Continues] BE --> AT %% Tryptophan Utilization AH --> BF{OR: Trp
Fate?} BF -->|Protein Synthesis| BG[tRNA-Trp Charging] BF -->|Biosynthesis| BH[NAD+ Synthesis via Kynurenine] BF -->|Secondary Metabolite| BI[Indole Production] BG --> BJ(\1) BH --> BK[NAD+ Cofactor] BI --> BL[Indole Signaling] BJ --> BM((\1)) BK --> BN((\1)) BL --> BO([Quorum Sensing]) E --> BP([Tryptophan Starvation]) AQ --> BP %% Styling %% Semantic Color Styling %% Semantic Color Styling %% name: Tryptophan Biosynthesis via Anthranilate
notes: Classic example of dual regulation (repression + attenuation). TrpA/B complex shows substrate channeling.
scientificAccuracy: Based on comprehensive biochemical and structural studies of tryptophan biosynthesis.

sources.title: The tryptophan operon of Escherichia coli.
Attenuation in the control of expression of bacterial operons.
Structural and functional analysis of anthranilate synthase from Escherichia coli: Insight into substrate recognition and allosteric regulation.
sources.authors: Morita K, Shimodaira N, Fujii H, Ishikawa K, Yanase M, Yamashita K, Sakuraba H, Kawakami T, Yamagishi A, Ohshima T.
